sections.paragraph:
m. with suff. שָׁרֵּךְ (from the root שָׁרַר)-

(1) nerve, muscle. Collect. Pro 3:8, רפְאוּת תְּהִי לְשָׁרֶּךָ “health (refreshment) shall it be to thy nerves” (in which is the seat of strength). In the other hemistich there is “to thy bones.”

(2) the navel (prop. the navel cord); Arab. سُرُّ Eze 16:4. Compare שֹׁרֶר.
